Default VPC only. Lambda functions run without VPC (public subnets access S3 directly via S3 endpoint or internet). No custom VPC networking.

cost_estimate.notes: Demo/dev account with very low traffic. Costs are estimates.

resources.name: image-upload-UploadFunction-iIIJ7xiZECuB
resources.description: Generates pre-signed S3 POST URL for direct browser-to-S3 upload (max 10MB). Uses SigV4 signature. Stores metadata as S3 object tags.

resources.name: image-upload-ListFilesFunction-Pb0dKq9dR0Is
resources.description: Lists objects in the image S3 bucket with optional prefix filter. Generates pre-signed view URLs inline. Supports maxKeys parameter (default 50).

resources.name: image-upload-GetViewUrlFunction-yMGI9X8Us5Em
resources.description: Generates a time-limited pre-signed GET URL (3600s) for viewing a specific image. Uses fileId as S3 key prefix.

resources.name: image-upload-DeleteFileFunction-EG7Cfj3m2P6f
resources.description: Deletes all S3 objects under a given fileId prefix. Uses SigV4 with virtual addressing.

resources.name: image-upload-api
resources.description: API for image upload service (IAM Auth)

resources.name: image-upload-imagebucket-t8isnbr8sswv
resources.notes: Stores images with UUID-based prefixes (fileId/filename). Object metadata stored as S3 tags. Max object size 10MB enforced by pre-signed POST policy.

resources.name: image-upload-websitebucket-vd866vxtcs1z
resources.notes: Hosts the single-page app (app.html). Uses S3 static website over HTTP (no HTTPS/CloudFront). Frontend uses AWS SigV4 request signing for API calls.

resources.name: appstream-app-settings-ap-southeast-2-535002891143-ar2b5jb0
resources.notes: Auto-created by AppStream 2.0. No active fleet in this account. Legacy resource from a past demo (ExampleStack-fleet, June 2025). Candidate for cleanup.

resources.name: appstream2-36fb080bb8-ap-southeast-2-535002891143-hwzroy6c
resources.notes: Auto-created by AppStream 2.0. No active fleet. Legacy resource from a past demo. Candidate for cleanup.

resources.purpose: Authenticates client SPA to API Gateway using AWS SigV4. Has execute-api:Invoke on the image-upload API.
resources.security_note: Long-lived static access key. This is the authentication mechanism for the static web frontend. No MFA. Candidate for refactoring to Cognito or short-lived credentials.

resources.description: Default key that protects my Lambda functions when no other key is defined
resources.notes: AWS-managed key (not customer-managed). Auto-created for Lambda. No migration action required; target platform will use its own key management.

not_present.eventbridge: None (service-linked role for CWE exists for SSM OpsItems only)

lambda_handlers.upload: app-code/lambda/upload/upload_handler.py (generates pre-signed S3 POST, SigV4, max 10MB)
lambda_handlers.list: app-code/lambda/list/list_handler.py (lists S3 objects, inline pre-signed URLs, supports prefix/maxKeys)
lambda_handlers.view: app-code/lambda/view/view_handler.py (generates pre-signed GET URL for a single file)
lambda_handlers.delete: app-code/lambda/delete/delete_handler.py (deletes all objects under fileId prefix)

source_code_findings.frontend: app-code/build/app.html (static SPA, calls API gateway with SigV4)

security_observations.finding: IAM user with static long-lived access key for API authentication
security_observations.detail: The SPA authenticates to API Gateway using AWS SigV4 with a static IAM access key (image-upload-api-user). Static credentials in browser context are a security risk.
security_observations.recommendation: Replace with Amazon Cognito Identity Pool or short-lived STS credentials.

security_observations.finding: S3 static website served over HTTP (no HTTPS)
security_observations.detail: WebsiteBucket uses S3 static website hosting without CloudFront, exposing the site over plain HTTP.
security_observations.recommendation: Serve via CloudFront with HTTPS. On Azure, Azure Static Web Apps provides HTTPS by default.

security_observations.finding: API Gateway using TLS 1.0 security policy
security_observations.detail: The API Gateway is configured with TLS_1_0, which includes deprecated cipher suites.
Multiple log groups from deleted stack versions (v2, older iterations) remain and incur storage costs.
security_observations.recommendation: Delete residual log groups after confirming they are no longer needed.

security_observations.detail: Stack drift status is NOT_CHECKED. Undrifted resources cannot be guaranteed.

summary.notes: Remnants of a decommissioned AppStream 2.0 environment also present (buckets + IAM roles + CW alarms)
